title: 02841_A_Watermark_for_Large_Language_Models
positive_examples: The watermark can mitigate potential harms of large language models by embedding signals into generated text that are algorithmically detectable, thus helping to combat malicious uses such as social engineering and election manipulation.
The watermarking framework allows for the detection of machine-generated text, which can help in identifying and reducing the spread of fake news and misinformation.
negative_examples: The paper discusses the risk of large language models being used for malicious purposes, including social engineering and election manipulation campaigns.
The proliferation of synthetic data complicates future dataset creation efforts, as synthetic data is often inferior to human content and must be detected and excluded before model training.
